rulings.Case Title: CAPITAL ONE BANK (USA), N.A. VS. BARBARA J. PLANK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 21, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF CAPITAL ONE BANK (USA), N.A. HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION. Denied. Judgment Debtor Barbara J. Plank has not demonstrated that she needs all of her earnings for the support of herself and her family. Her monthly income exceeds her monthly expenses. The Sheriff is directed to release any earnings to Judgment Creditor Capital One Bank (USA) N.A for payment on the judgment. The wages of Judgment Debtor Barbara J. Plank shall be garnished at $ 524.00 per pay period. rulings.Case Title: SHEREEN PINTO et al VS. M. GAEHWILER CONSTRUCTION,INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 21, 2014, Line 3, DEFENDANTs BARNECUT ROOFING COMPANY, INC. and GARY DEGLIANTONI's MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Motion to strike denied. As the ruling on Defendants' Demurrers provides, the Complaint sufficiently alleges a cause of action for willful misconduct. The required elements to establish malice based on conscious disregard are the same as those required to establish willful misconduct. (Scott v. Phoenix Schools, Inc. (2009) 175 Cal.App.4th 702.) Thus, Plaintiff may also claim punitive damages. (CC § 3294(a).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: SHEREEN PINTO et al VS. M. GAEHWILER CONSTRUCTION,INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Demur And Demur To Pltfs' 2nd Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 21, 2014, Line 3, DEFENDANT BARNECUT ROOFING COMPANY, INC.'s Demurrer To Plaintiffs' 2nd Amended Complaint. Demurrer overruled. A cause of action for willful misconduct requires allegations of actual or constructive knowledge of the peril to be apprehended; actual or constructive knowledge that injury is a probable, as opposed to a possible, result of the danger; and conscious failure to act to avoid the peril. (Bain v. Western Pacific R. Co. (1976) 56 Cal.App.3d 902, 905.) The Second Amended Complaint (SAC) alleges many specific actions taken by Defendants that are characterized as improper methods and that Defendants "knew, or as licensed contractors who specialized in waterproofing, should have known" that using these improper methods created the risk of leakage. (SAC 50.) The SAC also alleges that as specialized contractors, Defendants had knowledge that each of the improper installation methods, especially when occurring together, would make the likelihood of leakage probable. (SAC 51.) Finally, Plaintiffs allege that by taking these actions with the knowledge of the risk created and the probability of its occurrence, Defendants consciously failed to avoid the harm. Therefore, Plaintiffs sufficiently allege the requisite elements of a willful misconduct cause of action. Moreover, the Plaintiffs allege that Defendant Degliantoni was an officer or director of Defendant Barnecut, and that he was personally guilty of the willful misconduct, thereby sufficiently alleging liability on the part of both Defendant Degliantoni as an individual and Defendant Barnecut for the willful misconduct of its corporate officer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: MOHSIN AHMMED VS. BANK OF AMERICA N.A. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Mohsin Ahmmed'S Motion To Compel Compliance With Court'S Prior Order Of Feb 25, 2014 Be Deft Bank Of America N.A. And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, May 21 2014, Line 1, Plaintiff Mohsin Ahmmed'S Motion To Compel Compliance With Court'S Prior Order Of Feb 25, 2014 Be Defendant Bank Of America N.A. And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge James Weixel, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion and request for sanctions are denied in all respects. The only matter at issue is Bank of America's compliance with the Court's order of February 25, 2014, which compelled further responses to "each request [for production of documents] at issue" in Plaintiff's previous motion. Bank of America served those further responses on March 5, 2014. The responses complied with the order. The lack of verification does not represent noncompliance, as the further responses simply cured the previous failure to indicate by Bates number the documents responsive to each of those 33 requests, and did not contain new facts for which verification was required. At any rate, the declaration of Scott Horowitz in opposition to the motion provides verification of that information and, in the Court's view, thus cures any possible deficiencies in that respect. Furthermore, the Court's previous order striking Bank of America's opposition did not operate as a bar or waiver of all objections. Therefore, it was not improper for Bank of America to interpose objections in its further responses. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to appeals@jimweixel.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DILLINGER CARTER & ASSOCIATES INC., VS. TIMOTHY C. VU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT (Adjudication)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 21, 2014, Line 5, DEFENDANT SHERRI KING, FORMERLY DBA SHERRI KING TAX SERVICES' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT (Adjudication). Defendant's motion for summary adjudication as to its first, third, and fourth causes of action is denied. Plaintiff has raised a triable issue as to whether Defendant's alleged concealment discharged Plaintiff' s obligation to pay amount under the promissory note. (See Plaintiff's Material Facts No. 7.) There is a triable issue regarding Defendant's classification as an employee or independent contractor; and thus Plaintiff's authority to deduct Defendant's ages for bookkeeping services. (See S.G. Borello & Sons, Inc. v. Dept. of Indus. Relations (1989) 48 Cal.3d 341 - "the determination of employee or independent-contractor status is one of fact if dependent upon the resolution of disputed evidence or inferences.") Thus, there is a triable issue of fact whether a good faith dispute existed that would preclude Defendant's entitlement to waiting time penalties. (See Deposition of Sherri King, dated October 9, 2013, pp. 35:2 - 37:6.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: NETSANET HAILEMICHAEL VS. BANK OF THE WEST, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Pltf'S Compliance W/ The Court'S April 15, 2014 Orders Compelling The Depositions Of Pltf And 3rd Party Witnesses, And For An Award Of Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, May 21 2014, Line 2, DEFENDANT BANK OF THE WEST's Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Compliance W/ The Court'S April 15, 2014 Orders Compelling The Depositions Of Plaintiff And 3rd Party Witnesses, And For An Award Of Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge James Weixel, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is denied as untimely. The Court continued the trial date from May 5, 2014 to July 28, 2014. The order continuing trial specifically stated that "[n]o pre-trial dates (e.g. discovery or dispositive motion deadlines) are extended" by virtue of the continuance. (Order Granting Defendant Bank of the West's Motion to Continue Trial, filed May 1, 2014, p. 2:13-14.) No extension of the discovery motion cutoff has been sought or granted. (See Code Civ. Proc., § 2024.050, subd. (a).) The motion is thus untimely. (Code Civ. Proc., § 2024.020.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to appeals@jimweixel.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: L&A WONG FAMILY PROPERTY ASSOCIATION LLC, et al VS. HAROLD WONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of And Mtn To Quash Service Of Subpoena Duces Tecum
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, May 21 2014, Line 4, DEFENDANT HAROLD WONG's Motion To Quash Service Of Subpoena Duces Tecum Pro Tem Judge James Weixel, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted. Plaintiffs did not serve, or cause to be served, a notice to consumer. (Code Civ. Proc., § 1985.3.) The statement in Plaintiffs' counsel's declaration that "upon information and belief" American Express/Datamark's agent informed counsel that a "notice" had been provided to Defendant regarding the subpoena is inadmissible hearsay. (Star Motors Imports, Inc. v. Superior Court (1979) 88 Cal.App.3d 201, 204.) The declaration also does not specify what "notice" was allegedly provided, i.e. whether it was the notice required by section 1985.3. The statute's language is mandatory. (See § 1985.3, subd. (b) [providing that the subpoenaing party "shall" give notice to the consumer or cause notice to be given].) Therefore, compliance with the notice provision is required before a consumer's records can be produced. Since the notice was not served here, the subpoena must be quashed. The timeliness of the motion is not an issue; the Court may entertain a motion to quash even after the date set for production. (Slagle v. Superior Court (1989), 211 Cal.App.3d 1309, 1312.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to appeals@jimweixel.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SEMEYON GOFMAN VS. WHITTIER ENTERPRISE LLC, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Attorney Fees
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 21, 2014, Line 9, DEFENDANTs WHITTIER ENTERPRISE LLC, and ALEX MESEONZNIK's Motion For Attorney Fees. The motion for fees is denied. The court determines that the motion is timely under CRC 3.1702 because neither plaintiff nor the clerk of the court served a notice of entry of dismissal on plaintiff. Accordingly, the 180-day time period never commenced. The court denies fees under Labor Code sec. 218.5 because defendant fails to demonstrate that plaintiff commenced the action in bad faith. The court denies fees under Gov't Code sec. 12965 because defendant fails to demonstrate that the FEHA claims were frivolous. rulings.Case Title: ALBERTO PEDRUCO VS. JENNIFER MATTHEWS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike Complaint Pursuant To Code Of Civ Proc 425.16;
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 21, 2014, Line 11, DEFENDANT JENNIFER MATTHEWS' Special Motion To Strike Complaint Pursuant To Code Of Civ Proc 425.16. Granted, in part, and denied, in part. Defendant Jennifer Mathews has met her initial burden to show that she engaged in protected activity by sending the two emails pertaining to her complaint to Human Resources. (CCP § 425.16(e).) Defendant's emails are not protected by the litigation privilege. (Civ. Code § 47(b).) For the first cause of action for defamation, Plaintiff has met his shifting burden of showing a probability of prevailing of the merits. Defendant's own evidence demonstrates that the contents of the two emails were false; Defendant was specifically reprimanded by Human Resources for sending these emails. (Mathews Decl., Exhibits B - E.) The false and unprivileged publication of these emails to 200 members of the San Francisco Division of the United Fire Service Women's Organization has a tendency to injure Plaintiff in his occupation. (Civ. Code § 45.) For the second cause of action, Plaintiff has not met his shifting burden of showing a probability of prevailing on the merits. Plaintiff has not proffered any evidence to support his intentional infliction of emotional distress claims. (See Pedruco Decl.) Defendant's objections are overruled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA HEWLETT VS. JAMES M BRADEN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 21, 2014, Line 14, DEFENDANT JAMES BRADEN's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend because plaintiff cannot establish causation or damages as a matter of law. The court takes judicial notice of the federal filings. The order dismissing appeal issued by Judge Breyer expressly found that there was no basis to appeal. The Ninth Circuit Court of Appeals affirmed that finding and similarly found that plaintiff's appeal was frivolous. "[A] client claiming that his [her] attorney was negligent in connection with litigation has the burden of proving that damages resulted, this burden involving, usually, the difficult task of demonstrating that, but for the negligence complained of, the client would have been successful in the prosecution or defense of the action in question." Sukoff v. Lemkin (1988) 202 Cal.App.3d 740, 744. In this case, even if defendant owed a duty to file an appeal, there was nothing he could have done to lead to a better outcome for plaintiff.
